claim_policy.rule: Risk signals are not treated as final target claims without authoritative source evidence, identifier confidence and scope proof.
claim_policy.memo_language: Use source, source_status, checked_at, freshness, fallback_used, confidence, limitation and diligence question for every memo claim.

cyber_infrastructure.title: Cyber infrastructure evidence
cyber_infrastructure.summary: 1 infrastructure item(s) have passive exposure signals but insufficient ownership proof.
cyber_infrastructure.source: Cloudflare DoH + Shodan InternetDB + IANA/RIR RDAP

rows.claim: 77.111.243.68 has ports 80, 443; no CVE signal returned, but ownership is UNPROVEN.
rows.limitation: The IP may be shared, hosted, historical, or otherwise not controlled by the target.
rows.diligence_question: Request asset inventory or hosting contract evidence before treating this as target exposure.

rows.claim: 2a02:2350:5:113:800a:86b0:19a3:92da returned no passive port or CVE exposure signal.
rows.limitation: InternetDB is passive and point-in-time; absence of data is not a clean security attestation.
rows.diligence_question: Confirm the external attack surface with seller-provided asset inventory.

diligence_questions: Request asset inventory or hosting contract evidence before treating this as target exposure.
Confirm the external attack surface with seller-provided asset inventory.

finance_forensics.title: Finance forensic evidence
finance_forensics.summary: Finance forensic evidence for True Legends IT B.V. verifies the Benford analyzer route, but no target-specific Benford conclusion is available yet.
finance_forensics.source: DueSight Financial Bridge + Benford Analyzer + DE Register Filing Gate

rows.claim: Benford analyzer route completed for True Legends IT B.V. on a deterministic smoke population. Only 0 target-specific finance values were extractable, below the 30-value minimum. Route-smoke metrics: chi2=0.0282, MAD=0.0142, n=64, deviation=LOW.
rows.limitation: This verifies that the Benford analyzer path works; it is not a target-specific finance finding and cannot support a memo conclusion.
rows.diligence_question: Collect an official filing, XBRL extract, P&L export or transaction-level population before using Benford as target evidence.

diligence_questions: Collect an official filing, XBRL extract, P&L export or transaction-level population before using Benford as target evidence.

rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Local OFAC/sanctions DuckDB is present with 19050 sanctions records.
rows.limitation: Inventory row only; exact entity/person screening still requires deterministic matching output.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Local registry DuckDB is present with 2 cached registry rows.
rows.limitation: Small cache; not enough for final UBO/shareholder closure without target-specific registry extract or graph provider output.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Adverse-media cache is present with 19 files and 1 target cache hits.
rows.limitation: Cached no-hit supports guarded media context only; complete clearance requires source list, query scope and timestamp.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Whale tracker cache is present with 5 snapshots.
rows.limitation: Portfolio/institutional-flow context only; usually not material for private NL targets without a matched security/issuer.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: NVD/CVE cache is present with 13 files.
rows.limitation: Cache availability is not target exposure proof; combine with tech fingerprinting, asset ownership and CVE matching.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: DueSight master DuckDB is present with 120 cert/cache rows.
rows.limitation: Internal cache inventory only; does not replace target-specific primary evidence.
rows.diligence_question: Which target-specific module output turns this source availability into a memo-safe claim?

rows.claim: Award intelligence module is not present at the expected first-class module path.
rows.limitation: FT1000/revenue/distress building blocks exist, but the lost award_intel module must be rebuilt before award claims enter standard reports.
rows.diligence_question: Rebuild award_db_builder, shadow_pl_award_bridge, award_intel_tool and seed_data before using award/gazelle claims.

rows.claim: No regulatory-register hit was found for True Legends IT B.V. in the checked routes.
rows.limitation: No-hit is not proof that no regulatory exposure exists; source availability, country scope and name matching limit coverage.
rows.diligence_question: Should the target be regulated based on activity, geography, license perimeter or group structure?

rows.claim: No target-relevant adverse-media signal was found for True Legends IT B.V. in the checked sources.
rows.source: DueSight adverse-media source ladder (public news + legal metadata)
rows.limitation: No-hit is point-in-time and source-limited; it is not proof of clean reputation.
rows.diligence_question: Should buyer-side diligence add local-language, paid-media or litigation searches for this sector/geography?

diligence_questions: Request asset inventory or hosting contract evidence before treating this as target exposure.
Confirm the external attack surface with seller-provided asset inventory.
Confirm legal name, registration number, UBOs and directors before final compliance sign-off.
Are UBOs, directors and material related parties also in scope for sanctions/PEP screening?
Collect an official filing, XBRL extract, P&L export or transaction-level population before using Benford as target evidence.
Retry official KvK Basisprofiel/Dataservice or a verified LEI/register source before memo reliance.
Which STAK, foreign-entity, missing-data or circular-ownership issue needs manual diligence before memo completion?
Should the target be regulated based on activity, geography, license perimeter or group structure?
Should buyer-side diligence add local-language, paid-media or litigation searches for this sector/geography?
Which profile claims are official-source confirmed versus inferred from search/news?
